hypothesis: If a single score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state trajectories and used with score-based (isodensity) interpolation during the reverse SDE followed by Score-Aligned Ascent refinement using the conditional score difference as an approximate reaction coordinate, then it will generate transition-state configurations with committor values near 0.5 and higher success rates and sampling efficiency than linear and geodesic interpolation baselines across toy potentials, alanine dipeptide, chignolin, and a chemical reaction.
